settings.ggJXnLEnRqH_text: What to look for in a face moisturiser for dry skin
settings.ggx-ifyHSbR_text: Products for dry skin: Ingredients to avoid
settings.ggGAJgV9tWf_text: Skincare for a dry face: Final tips
settings.ggsuc8uMOvV_text:
Dry skin isn’t just a cosmetic concern. It’s a sign that your face is lacking essential hydration and nutrients. Over time, this can weaken your skin barrier, increase sensitivity, and make fine lines appear more pronounced.

 

What many people don’t realize is that, even if you’re already using a moisturiser, you can still struggle with dry skin. A lightweight lotion may add moisture temporarily, but without the right balance of ingredients, it can evaporate quickly, leaving your face dry again.

 

In this guide, we’ll take a closer look at the ingredients you should look out for in a moisturiser for dry skin, as well as those you should avoid. We’ll also spotlight some of our favourite Korean moisturisers. These nourishing, barrier-first formulas are ideal for boosting hydration, reducing irritation, and helping dry skin glow again.
settings.ggw51K8muVj_text:
Dry skin isn’t always characterized by rough or flaky patches. Sometimes it’s:

  • Tightness, especially after cleansing
  • A dull complexion
  • Fine lines that come and go
  • Increasingly sensitive skin 
  • Redness that appears easily in windy or winter weather
  • A feeling that your moisturiser absorbs quickly but doesn’t last

 

These signs point to a weakened skin barrier. When your skin lacks enough natural oils, it struggles to hold onto moisture and protect itself from environmental stressors. As a result, hydration escapes more easily, and irritants, like harsh weather, impact your skin more easily.

 

It’s also important to understand that dry skin and dehydrated skin aren’t the same thing. If you have dry skin, your skin naturally produces less oil, which means your barrier needs consistent support. Dehydrated skin, on the other hand, means a lack of water, and this can affect any skin type.

 

Dehydration is often triggered by external factors such as cold weather, over-exfoliation, harsh cleansers, or air conditioning, and it can make dryness feel more intense. Read our guide to supporting hydrated skin to learn more.
settings.ggETGZ8Sv5y_text: What to look for in a face moisturiser for dry skin
settings.ggQU5aCn5I9_text:
To choose a moisturiser that helps repair the skin barrier, boost water retention, and soothe dryness, look out for ingredients like: 

  • Ceramides, which help strengthen the skin barrier and prevent moisture from escaping
  • Hyaluronic acid, which draws water into the skin to relieve tightness and improve hydration levels
  • Glycerin, which supports moisture retention and keeps skin feeling soft 
  • Squalane, which replaces lost oils and improves skin suppleness 
  • Shea butter, which deeply nourishes and helps protect against dryness caused by cold or windy weather
  • Panthenol, which soothes irritation and supports skin comfort while reinforcing hydration
settings.ggh7fP9vFam_text: Products for dry skin: Ingredients to avoid
settings.ggeEQJIOEvb_text:
To avoid choosing the wrong type of moisturiser, be careful with these ingredients, which can cause irritation, increase moisture loss, or make dryness feel worse:

  • High concentrations of alcohol which can strip the skin and leave it feeling tight or uncomfortable
  • Strong synthetic fragrance (usually listed as “fragrance,” “parfum,” or “aroma”) which may trigger sensitivity or redness, especially if your barrier is already weakened
  • Menthol or peppermint which may aggravate dryness and sensitivity
  • Witch hazel which can feel soothing but may be drying in some formulas
  • High levels of acid which can make flaking worse
settings.ggGZrJGgPQ__text: Ready to find the best moisturisers for dry skin? Here are some of our top Korean skincare picks.
settings.ggEb3LFnl-K_text: Beauty of Joseon Dynasty Cream is a rich, nourishing moisturiser designed to support dry and weakened skin barriers. Squalane helps replenish lost moisture while rice bran water and ginseng boost radiance and strengthen the skin. It’s creamy with a smooth, cushiony texture that feels comforting for dry skin.
settings.ggeAh5XSztk_text: Dr. Althea 345 Relief Cream is a calming moisturiser designed for dry and sensitive skin. It combines hydrating and soothing ingredients like panthenol and niacinamide to reduce redness and discomfort. The texture sits between a gel and a cream, absorbing smoothly without feeling greasy.
settings.ggRdar2RkDe_text: ETUDE SoonJung 2x Barrier Intensive Cream is an all-time favourite face cream for dry skin. It includes panthenol and madecassoside to calm irritation and squalane to prevent moisture loss. It’s also fragrance-free and designed to minimize potential irritants.
settings.ggO0mGMQrjT_text: IUNIK Centella Calming Gel Cream is a lightweight option that works well for skin that is just mildly dry. Infused with centella asiatica and hydrating ingredients, it helps soothe sensitivity and boost hydration. The gel-cream texture absorbs quickly and feels breathable on the skin, making it ideal for layering under SPF or makeup.
settings.ggycz9dDBu8_text: If your skin constantly feels tight no matter how much product you apply, ISNTREE Hyaluronic Acid Moist Cream could be a great option for you. It includes five different types of concentrated hyaluronic acid to help you lock in moisture.
settings.gg98Bxkm-11_text: Skincare for a dry face: Final tips
settings.ggusix--Lyp_text:
A healthy, glowing appearance starts with the right moisturiser. And for people with dry skin, this means barrier-supporting formulas, calming ingredients that reduce irritation, and a focus on locking in hydration. 

 

To help your moisturiser work as effectively as possible, focus on using it both morning and night, massaging it in thoroughly, and adding additional serums for hydration if needed. 

 

With your moisturiser doing the right work beneath the surface, your skin will feel more comfortable, balanced, and resilient in no time.
settings.ggiovkgPj-g_text: Can I use gel moisturiser for dry skin?
settings.ggIqWBTWyCh_text: You can, but it depends on how dry your skin is. For mild dryness, rich gel formulas can work really well. However, for severe dryness, creams are often more effective.
settings.ggmlPtbFRRS_text: Is Beauty of Joseon moisturiser good for dry skin?
settings.ggqkr4J3Gfj_text: Yes, Beauty of Joseon offers several creams that are ideal for dry skin with barrier-supporting ingredients such as squalane and ginseng.
settings.ggLMaq6qgBK_text: Which is the best moisturiser for dehydrated skin?
settings.ggt_E3-LLwF_text: For dehydrated skin, look for formulas rich in humectants like hyaluronic acid and glycerin. ISNTREE Hyaluronic Acid Moist Cream is a great example!
